Direct Debit - it's the easiest way to pay
Setup a Direct Debit by downloading the
Direct Debit Instruction (DDI) from our website
edfenergy.com/billing. Fill this in and send it to:
Freepost EDF CUSTOMER CORRESPONDENCE
BACS or CHAPS 
You can transfer money straight into our account
either by BACS (Bankers Automated Clearing
System) or CHAPS (Clearing House Automated
Payment System). Our account details are as follows.
Account Name: EDF Energy Customers Ltd
Sort Code: 40-05-30
Account number: 44151844
Bank: HSBC Bank plc, 60 Queen Street, London,
EC4N 4TR.
IBAN Number: GB25MIDL40053044151844
SWIFT Code: MIDLGB22
If you want to pay by BACS or CHAPS, you need
to arrange this through your bank. Once you have
arranged the transfer, let us know by sending an
email to remittanceadvices@edfenergy.com. 
Your email must quote your 10-digit EDF Energy
account number shown on the front of this bill.
For more information on how to pay, visit
edfenergy.com/billing-payments
Be aware of scams that falsely tell you about a
change in payment details or payment
method. If you’re unsure, please get in touch
with us to check.
Debit card or credit card
To pay by debit card or credit card, phone the
revenue management team on 0845 302 7113*.
Please allow ten working days for your payment
to be processed.
At a bank or building society
You can pay by cash or cheque at a bank or building
society. It can take up to ten working days for
the payment to reach us.
Please note, you may have to pay a fee at some
banks and building societies.
Get in touch
Got a question about your bill?
If you'd like to know how your bill is calculated,
visit edfenergy.com/billing or call our
Customer Services on the number shown
on the front page of this bill.
MyBusiness
You can also register for our free
MyBusiness service on our website 
mybusinessaccount.edfenergy.com.
This service lets you check your account
information, download your bills, give us a meter
reading and get consumption data.
Making a complaint
Please get in touch with your dedicated
customer services team if you have a complaint.
They will do everything they can to put things
right as quickly as possible. Go to
edfenergy.com/complaints for our full
complaints procedure.
If you're a small business or microbusiness
customer
If your complaint isn't sorted after eight weeks
or you've received a 'deadlock' letter, you can
get in touch with the Energy Ombudsman. The
service is free and independent, and any
decision the Ombudsman makes is binding on
us - not you.
Call 0330 440 1624 (Monday to Friday
8am-6pm); email
enquiry@energyombudsman.org; Visit
energyombudsman.org
If you're a small or microbusiness customer who
has appointed a TPI, they'll be a member of a
qualifying dispute settlement service and can
direct you to that service. If you're unable to
contact your TPI, you can contact us at
edfenergy.com/complaints
More support
Citizens Advice and Advice Direct Scotland
If you're a small business or microbusiness
consumer, you can get help with energy problems.
This includes issues with your bills or meters,
or if you're struggling to pay for your energy.
If you live in England or Wales, go to
citizensadvice.org.uk/energy or contact the
Citizens Advice consumer service on 0808 223
1133. Calls are free.
If you live in Scotland, go to cas.org.uk, or
contact your Local Citizens Advice office. If you
live in Scotland, you can also contact Advice
Direct Scotland on 0808 196 8660 or visit
energyadvice.scot Calls are free.
If you need specialist support to resolve your
energy problem, Citizens Advice and Advice
Direct Scotland can refer you directly to the
Extra Help Unit Service.
Small business definition
• Fewer than 50 employees or their full-time
equivalent and an annual turnover of at most £6.5
million or a balance sheet total of £5.0 million. or
• An annual consumption of electricity of not
more than 200,000 kWh or
• An annual consumption of gas of not more than
500,000 kWh.
This document is fully recyclable
The paper used is made from 
50% recycled waste paper and
50% fibre from well-managed
natural resources.
Useful information
For more information on our products
and services, go to the website at
www.edfenergy.com/largebusiness.
Helping Britain's business and public sector
achieve Net Zero. For more information visit
edfenergy.com/net-zero-business
Site access
We take the safety of all our customers very
seriously. That’s why our representatives who visit
customers are highly trained and always carry
identification. If you’d like more information on
our obligations when visiting premises, phone us
on 0845 366 3664*.
Electricity and gas theft
Energy theft, carried out by interfering with
meters or connections, increases costs to our
customers. This means higher bills, which affects
everyone. Energy theft is a criminal offence
and can be extremely dangerous. If you suspect
someone is stealing electricity or gas, or both,
call the stayenergysafe reporting line on
0800 023 2777 or via stayenergysafe.co.uk. It
will be looked into urgently and your call can be
kept confidential. Alternatively, contact us directly
on 0333 200 5100.

Microbusiness definition
• An annual consumption of electricity of less than
  100,000 kWh or
• An annual consumption of gas of less than
  293,000 kWh or
• Fewer than the equivalent of ten full time
   employees and
• An annual turnover or annual balance sheet
   total not exceeding £2 million
Are you moving?
If you’re moving premises, let us know by filling in 
the form on our website
edfenergy.com/movingbusiness, or you can 
phone 0845 366 3664* for more advice.
Please have the MPAN or MPR numbers shown on 
your bill to hand so we can deal with your enquiry. 
You'll need to give us written notice at least 28 days 
before you move so that we can make sure your 
final bill is accurate. You'll also need to provide a
final meter reading and a forwarding address.
Do you need a new supply business 
connection?
The easiest way to set up a new connection is by 
filling in the form on our website
edfenergy.com/new-connection. Or email 
newsupply@edfenergy.com. Alternatively, call us 
on 0845 366 3664*.

The small print
Your Privacy
We respect your privacy and are committed to
providing you with a clear understanding of how we
use your data. To find out about the ways in which
we protect and use your data, please visit our Privacy
Notice and Policy at edfenergy.com/yourprivacy
If you’d like a hard copy of the privacy policy you can
contact us on 0333 200 5100 and we’ll post it to you.
VAT
Value added tax (VAT) is charged on your energy
bills . If you can claim VAT relief for domestic or
charitable non-business use, you need to fill in a
declaration form, which is available on our website.
For more information on VAT relief and the
declaration you must make, visit HMRC’s website
at HMRC.gov.uk.
Climate Change Levy (CCL)
Climate Change Levy (CCL) is a tax on the energy
used by businesses. It aims to encourage users to
be more energy efficient and reduce waste. Your
CCL charge will take account of any PP11 Supplier
certificate you have. Our CCL registration number
is: 523 0412 02 0000.
For more information on CCL, visit the website at
HMRC.gov.uk.
EDF Energy
EDF Energy is a trading name used by EDF Energy
Customers Ltd, Reg. No. 02228297 whose
Registered Office is at Nova North, 11 Bressenden Place,
London, SW1E 5BY, incorporated in England and Wales.
EDF Energy Customers Ltd is responsible for
meeting the supply obligations for all EDF supply
contracts.
Smart metering for business
We’re now introducing smart meters for our non
half-hourly business customers, which can help you
save time and provide more accurate bills from
automated meter readings. For more information,
visit edfenergy.com/smartmeter-business
Access to your consumption data

If your consumption data is not available, please

By learning about your energy usage, you can
potentially lower your carbon footprint and your bills.

For emergencies
Power cuts
Check your trip switch to make sure it's switched on. If it's
off, try switching it back on. If the switch moves back to
the off position you may have a fault with an appliance
or circuit in your property.
Do not touch any fuses or trip switches that show signs
of burning or if there is any black tar leaking. If this is the
case, or if your trip switch is on but you have no power
anywhere in your property then please call the emergency
number shown at the top of page 1.
Extending or Terminating your contract
If you're on a fixed or flexible contract, the 
Charges stated in your Contract Information 
Pack (CIP) will automatically end on the 
Earliest Termination Date shown in your CIP. 
You may write to us at any time up to the 
Earliest Termination Date to confirm that
you don't want to be transferred onto our
Extended Supply Charges and would like to
switch to a different supplier. If we don't 
hear from you by the Earliest Termination 
Date, you'll automatically move onto our 
Extended Supply Charges. You'll remain on
Extended Supply Charges until you either
agree another supply contract with us or
transfer to another supplier, which you can
do at no cost to you (as long as you have
no outstanding balance on your account). 
These Extended Supply Charges are variable
and typically higher than the charges found
in your CIP.
*All phone calls to and from us may be recorded for monitoring or training purposes. Calls to 0845 numbers will cost up to 7p per minute plus your standard network charges. 
The price of calls may vary with other operators and calls from mobiles may be considerably higher. Please check with your operator for exact charges.

• An annual consumption of gas of not more than
500,000 kWh.
This document is fully recyclable
The paper used is made from 
50% recycled waste paper and
50% fibre from well-managed
natural resources.
Useful information
For more information on our products
and services, go to the website at
www.edfenergy.com/largebusiness.
Helping Britain's business and public sector
achieve Net Zero. For more information visit
edfenergy.com/net-zero-business
Site access
We take the safety of all our customers very
seriously. That’s why our representatives who visit
customers are highly trained and always carry
identification. If you’d like more information on
our obligations when visiting premises, phone us
on 0845 366 3664*.
Electricity and gas theft
Energy theft, carried out by interfering with
meters or connections, increases costs to our
customers. This means higher bills, which affects
everyone. Energy theft is a criminal offence
and can be extremely dangerous. If you suspect
someone is stealing electricity or gas, or both,
call the stayenergysafe reporting line on
0800 023 2777 or via stayenergysafe.co.uk. It
will be looked into urgently and your call can be
kept confidential. Alternatively, contact us directly
on 0333 200 5100.
Payment Processing Centre
PO Box 140
Plymouth
PL3 5RG
Page 7 of 8
There's another page with details of what to do if you have a powercut and our fuel mix detail.
Microbusiness definition
• An annual consumption of electricity of less than
  100,000 kWh or
• An annual consumption of gas of less than
  293,000 kWh or
• Fewer than the equivalent of ten full time
   employees and
• An annual turnover or annual balance sheet
   total not exceeding £2 million
Are you moving?
If you’re moving premises, let us know by filling in 
the form on our website
edfenergy.com/movingbusiness, or you can 
phone 0845 366 3664* for more advice.
Please have the MPAN or MPR numbers shown on 
your bill to hand so we can deal with your enquiry. 
You'll need to give us written notice at least 28 days 
before you move so that we can make sure your 
final bill is accurate. You'll also need to provide a
final meter reading and a forwarding address.
Do you need a new supply business 
connection?
The easiest way to set up a new connection is by 
filling in the form on our website
edfenergy.com/new-connection. Or email 
newsupply@edfenergy.com. Alternatively, call us 
on 0845 366 3664*.

The small print
Your Privacy
We respect your privacy and are committed to
providing you with a clear understanding of how we
use your data. To find out about the ways in which
we protect and use your data, please visit our Privacy
Notice and Policy at edfenergy.com/yourprivacy
If you’d like a hard copy of the privacy policy you can
contact us on 0333 200 5100 and we’ll post it to you.
VAT
Value added tax (VAT) is charged on your energy
bills . If you can claim VAT relief for domestic or
charitable non-business use, you need to fill in a
declaration form, which is available on our website.
For more information on VAT relief and the
declaration you must make, visit HMRC’s website
at HMRC.gov.uk.
Climate Change Levy (CCL)
Climate Change Levy (CCL) is a tax on the energy
used by businesses. It aims to encourage users to
be more energy efficient and reduce waste. Your
CCL charge will take account of any PP11 Supplier
certificate you have. Our CCL registration number
is: 523 0412 02 0000.
For more information on CCL, visit the website at
HMRC.gov.uk.
EDF Energy
EDF Energy is a trading name used by EDF Energy
Customers Ltd, Reg. No. 02228297 whose
Registered Office is at Nova North, 11 Bressenden Place,
London, SW1E 5BY, incorporated in England and Wales.
EDF Energy Customers Ltd is responsible for
meeting the supply obligations for all EDF supply
contracts.
Smart metering for business
We’re now introducing smart meters for our non
half-hourly business customers, which can help you
save time and provide more accurate bills from
automated meter readings. For more information,
visit edfenergy.com/smartmeter-business
Access to your consumption data
You can access your data by logging into your
MyBusiness account:
edfenergy.com/mybusiness
If your consumption data is not available, please
contact your customer service team (information on
top of your invoice).
By learning about your energy usage, you can
potentially lower your carbon footprint and your bills.
Payment Processing Centre
PO Box 140
Plymouth
PL3 5RG
Page 8 of 8
Our fuel mix   
Every year we must publish details of the fuel
sources that have been used to generate the 
electricity we supply to our customers.
The information in the table below covers our supply 
licence for EDF Energy Customers Ltd for the period 
from April 2024 to March 2025.
Our customers’ electricity is sourced from our own
UK power stations, the wholesale energy market
and other independent power generators. We
are a major supporter of independent renewable 
generators.
The figures for UK average fuel mix are provided by
the Department for Energy Security & Net Zero (DESNZ).
Depending on the tariff you are on, the
fuel source and carbon emissions associated with the
generation of your electricity may vary.
For more information on our fuel mix, visit 
edfenergy.com/fuelmix

fuel mix 4.2% 21.0% 54.8% 18.2% 1.8% 135 0.0038

This document is fully recyclable
The paper used is made from 
50% recycled waste paper and 
50% fibre from well-managed
natural resources.
For emergencies
Power cuts
Check your trip switch to make sure it's switched on. If it's
off, try switching it back on. If the switch moves back to
the off position you may have a fault with an appliance
or circuit in your property.
Do not touch any fuses or trip switches that show signs
of burning or if there is any black tar leaking. If this is the
case, or if your trip switch is on but you have no power
anywhere in your property then please call the emergency
number shown at the top of page 1.
Extending or Terminating your contract
If you're on a fixed or flexible contract, the 
Charges stated in your Contract Information 
Pack (CIP) will automatically end on the 
Earliest Termination Date shown in your CIP. 
You may write to us at any time up to the 
Earliest Termination Date to confirm that
you don't want to be transferred onto our
Extended Supply Charges and would like to
switch to a different supplier. If we don't 
hear from you by the Earliest Termination 
Date, you'll automatically move onto our 
Extended Supply Charges. You'll remain on
Extended Supply Charges until you either
agree another supply contract with us or
transfer to another supplier, which you can
do at no cost to you (as long as you have
no outstanding balance on your account). 
These Extended Supply Charges are variable
and typically higher than the charges found
in your CIP.
*All phone calls to and from us may be recorded for monitoring or training purposes. Calls to 0845 numbers will cost up to 7p per minute plus your standard network charges. 
The price of calls may vary with other operators and calls from mobiles may be considerably higher. Please check with your operator for exact charges.

For emergencies
Power cuts
Check your trip switch to make sure it's switched on. If it's
off, try switching it back on. If the switch moves back to
the off position you may have a fault with an appliance
or circuit in your property.
Do not touch any fuses or trip switches that show signs
of burning or if there is any black tar leaking. If this is the
case, or if your trip switch is on but you have no power
anywhere in your property then please call the emergency
number shown at the top of page 1.
Extending or Terminating your contract
If you're on a fixed or flexible contract, the 
Charges stated in your Contract Information 
Pack (CIP) will automatically end on the 
Earliest Termination Date shown in your CIP. 
You may write to us at any time up to the 
Earliest Termination Date to confirm that
you don't want to be transferred onto our
Extended Supply Charges and would like to
switch to a different supplier. If we don't 
hear from you by the Earliest Termination 
Date, you'll automatically move onto our 
Extended Supply Charges. You'll remain on
Extended Supply Charges until you either
agree another supply contract with us or
transfer to another supplier, which you can
do at no cost to you (as long as you have
no outstanding balance on your account). 
These Extended Supply Charges are variable
and typically higher than the charges found
in your CIP.
*All phone calls to and from us may be recorded for monitoring or training purposes. Calls to 0845 numbers will cost up to 7p per minute plus your standard network charges. 
The price of calls may vary with other operators and calls from mobiles may be considerably higher. Please check with your operator for exact charges.

Page 6 of 6
Our fuel mix   
Every year we must publish details of the fuel
sources that have been used to generate the 
electricity we supply to our customers.
The information in the table below covers our supply 
licence for EDF Energy Customers Ltd for the period 
from April 2024 to March 2025.
Our customers’ electricity is sourced from our own
UK power stations, the wholesale energy market
and other independent power generators. We
are a major supporter of independent renewable 
generators.
The figures for UK average fuel mix are provided by
the Department for Energy Security & Net Zero (DESNZ).
Depending on the tariff you are on, the
fuel source and carbon emissions associated with the
generation of your electricity may vary.
For more information on our fuel mix, visit 
edfenergy.com/fuelmix
 
EDF's
fuel mix 4.2% 21.0% 54.8% 18.2% 1.8% 135 0.0038
Contribution to our
carbon emissions 28.3% 59.3% 0.0% 0.0% 12.4%
UK average
fuel mix 5.9% 33.3% 16.2% 42.1% 2.5% 154 0.0011
This document is fully recyclable
The paper used is made from 
50% recycled waste paper and 
50% fibre from well-managed
natural resources.
For emergencies
Power cuts
Check your trip switch to make sure it's switched on. If it's
off, try switching it back on. If the switch moves back to
the off position you may have a fault with an appliance
or circuit in your property.
Do not touch any fuses or trip switches that show signs
of burning or if there is any black tar leaking. If this is the
case, or if your trip switch is on but you have no power
anywhere in your property then please call the emergency
number shown at the top of page 1.
Extending or Terminating your contract
If you're on a fixed or flexible contract, the 
Charges stated in your Contract Information 
Pack (CIP) will automatically end on the 
Earliest Termination Date shown in your CIP. 
You may write to us at any time up to the 
Earliest Termination Date to confirm that
you don't want to be transferred onto our
Extended Supply Charges and would like to
switch to a different supplier. If we don't 
hear from you by the Earliest Termination 
Date, you'll automatically move onto our 
Extended Supply Charges. You'll remain on
Extended Supply Charges until you either
agree another supply contract with us or
transfer to another supplier, which you can
do at no cost to you (as long as you have
no outstanding balance on your account). 
These Extended Supply Charges are variable
and typically higher than the charges found
in your CIP.
*All phone calls to and from us may be recorded for monitoring or training purposes. Calls to 0845 numbers will cost up to 7p per minute plus your standard network charges. 
The price of calls may vary with other operators and calls from mobiles may be considerably higher. Please check with your operator for exact charges.

How to pay
Direct Debit - it's the easiest way to pay
Setup a Direct Debit by downloading the
Direct Debit Instruction (DDI) from our website
edfenergy.com/billing. Fill this in and send it to:
Freepost EDF CUSTOMER CORRESPONDENCE
BACS or CHAPS 
You can transfer money straight into our account
either by BACS (Bankers Automated Clearing
System) or CHAPS (Clearing House Automated
Payment System). Our account details are as follows.
Account Name: EDF Energy Customers Ltd
Sort Code: 40-05-30
Account number: 44151844
Bank: HSBC Bank plc, 60 Queen Street, London,
EC4N 4TR.
IBAN Number: GB25MIDL40053044151844
SWIFT Code: MIDLGB22
If you want to pay by BACS or CHAPS, you need
to arrange this through your bank. Once you have
arranged the transfer, let us know by sending an
email to remittanceadvices@edfenergy.com. 
Your email must quote your 10-digit EDF Energy
account number shown on the front of this bill.
For more information on how to pay, visit
edfenergy.com/billing-payments
Be aware of scams that falsely tell you about a
change in payment details or payment
method. If you’re unsure, please get in touch
with us to check.
Debit card or credit card
To pay by debit card or credit card, phone the
revenue management team on 0845 302 7113*.
Please allow ten working days for your payment
to be processed.
At a bank or building society
You can pay by cash or cheque at a bank or building
society. It can take up to ten working days for
the payment to reach us.
Please note, you may have to pay a fee at some
banks and building societies.
Get in touch
Got a question about your bill?
If you'd like to know how your bill is calculated,
visit edfenergy.com/billing or call our
Customer Services on the number shown
on the front page of this bill.
MyBusiness
You can also register for our free
MyBusiness service on our website 
mybusinessaccount.edfenergy.com.
This service lets you check your account
information, download your bills, give us a meter
reading and get consumption data.
Making a complaint
Please get in touch with your dedicated
customer services team if you have a complaint.
They will do everything they can to put things
right as quickly as possible. Go to
edfenergy.com/complaints for our full
complaints procedure.
If you're a small business or microbusiness
customer
If your complaint isn't sorted after eight weeks
or you've received a 'deadlock' letter, you can
get in touch with the Energy Ombudsman. The
service is free and independent, and any
decision the Ombudsman makes is binding on
us - not you.
Call 0330 440 1624 (Monday to Friday
8am-6pm); email
enquiry@energyombudsman.org; Visit
energyombudsman.org
If you're a small or microbusiness customer who
has appointed a TPI, they'll be a member of a
qualifying dispute settlement service and can
direct you to that service. If you're unable to
contact your TPI, you can contact us at
edfenergy.com/complaints
More support
Citizens Advice and Advice Direct Scotland
If you're a small business or microbusiness
consumer, you can get help with energy problems.
This includes issues with your bills or meters,
or if you're struggling to pay for your energy.
If you live in England or Wales, go to
citizensadvice.org.uk/energy or contact the
Citizens Advice consumer service on 0808 223
1133. Calls are free.
If you live in Scotland, go to cas.org.uk, or
contact your Local Citizens Advice office. If you
live in Scotland, you can also contact Advice
Direct Scotland on 0808 196 8660 or visit
energyadvice.scot Calls are free.
If you need specialist support to resolve your
energy problem, Citizens Advice and Advice
Direct Scotland can refer you directly to the
Extra Help Unit Service.
Small business definition
• Fewer than 50 employees or their full-time
equivalent and an annual turnover of at most £6.5
million or a balance sheet total of £5.0 million. or
• An annual consumption of electricity of not
more than 200,000 kWh or
• An annual consumption of gas of not more than
500,000 kWh.
This document is fully recyclable
The paper used is made from 
50% recycled waste paper and
50% fibre from well-managed
natural resources.
Useful information
For more information on our products
and services, go to the website at
www.edfenergy.com/largebusiness.
Helping Britain's business and public sector
achieve Net Zero. For more information visit
edfenergy.com/net-zero-business
Site access
We take the safety of all our customers very
seriously. That’s why our representatives who visit
customers are highly trained and always carry
identification. If you’d like more information on
our obligations when visiting premises, phone us
on 0845 366 3664*.
Electricity and gas theft
Energy theft, carried out by interfering with
meters or connections, increases costs to our
customers. This means higher bills, which affects
everyone. Energy theft is a criminal offence
and can be extremely dangerous. If you suspect
someone is stealing electricity or gas, or both,
call the stayenergysafe reporting line on
0800 023 2777 or via stayenergysafe.co.uk. It
will be looked into urgently and your call can be
kept confidential. Alternatively, contact us directly
on 0333 200 5100.
Payment Processing Centre
PO Box 140
Plymouth
PL3 5RG
Page 6 of 7
There's another page with details of what to do if you have a powercut and our fuel mix detail.
Microbusiness definition
• An annual consumption of electricity of less than
  100,000 kWh or
• An annual consumption of gas of less than
  293,000 kWh or
• Fewer than the equivalent of ten full time
   employees and
• An annual turnover or annual balance sheet
   total not exceeding £2 million
Are you moving?
If you’re moving premises, let us know by filling in 
the form on our website
edfenergy.com/movingbusiness, or you can 
phone 0845 366 3664* for more advice.
Please have the MPAN or MPR numbers shown on 
your bill to hand so we can deal with your enquiry. 
You'll need to give us written notice at least 28 days 
before you move so that we can make sure your 
final bill is accurate. You'll also need to provide a
final meter reading and a forwarding address.
Do you need a new supply business 
connection?
The easiest way to set up a new connection is by 
filling in the form on our website
edfenergy.com/new-connection. Or email 
newsupply@edfenergy.com. Alternatively, call us 
on 0845 366 3664*.

The small print
Your Privacy
We respect your privacy and are committed to
providing you with a clear understanding of how we
use your data. To find out about the ways in which
we protect and use your data, please visit our Privacy
Notice and Policy at edfenergy.com/yourprivacy
If you’d like a hard copy of the privacy policy you can
contact us on 0333 200 5100 and we’ll post it to you.
VAT
Value added tax (VAT) is charged on your energy
bills . If you can claim VAT relief for domestic or
charitable non-business use, you need to fill in a
declaration form, which is available on our website.
For more information on VAT relief and the
declaration you must make, visit HMRC’s website
at HMRC.gov.uk.
Climate Change Levy (CCL)
Climate Change Levy (CCL) is a tax on the energy
used by businesses. It aims to encourage users to
be more energy efficient and reduce waste. Your
CCL charge will take account of any PP11 Supplier
certificate you have. Our CCL registration number
is: 523 0412 02 0000.
For more information on CCL, visit the website at
HMRC.gov.uk.
EDF Energy
EDF Energy is a trading name used by EDF Energy
Customers Ltd, Reg. No. 02228297 whose
Registered Office is at Nova North, 11 Bressenden Place,
London, SW1E 5BY, incorporated in England and Wales.
EDF Energy Customers Ltd is responsible for
meeting the supply obligations for all EDF supply
contracts.
Smart metering for business
We’re now introducing smart meters for our non
half-hourly business customers, which can help you
save time and provide more accurate bills from
automated meter readings. For more information,
visit edfenergy.com/smartmeter-business
Access to your consumption data
You can access your data by logging into your
MyBusiness account:
edfenergy.com/mybusiness
If your consumption data is not available, please
contact your customer service team (information on
top of your invoice).
By learning about your energy usage, you can
potentially lower your carbon footprint and your bills.
Payment Processing Centre
PO Box 140
Plymouth
PL3 5RG
Page 7 of 7
Our fuel mix   
Every year we must publish details of the fuel
sources that have been used to generate the 
electricity we supply to our customers.
The information in the table below covers our supply 
licence for EDF Energy Customers Ltd for the period 
from April 2024 to March 2025.
Our customers’ electricity is sourced from our own
UK power stations, the wholesale energy market
and other independent power generators. We
are a major supporter of independent renewable 
generators.
The figures for UK average fuel mix are provided by
the Department for Energy Security & Net Zero (DESNZ).
Depending on the tariff you are on, the
fuel source and carbon emissions associated with the
generation of your electricity may vary.
For more information on our fuel mix, visit 
edfenergy.com/fuelmix
 
EDF's
fuel mix 4.2% 21.0% 54.8% 18.2% 1.8% 135 0.0038
Contribution to our
carbon emissions 28.3% 59.3% 0.0% 0.0% 12.4%
UK average
fuel mix 5.9% 33.3% 16.2% 42.1% 2.5% 154 0.0011
This document is fully recyclable
The paper used is made from 
50% recycled waste paper and 
50% fibre from well-managed
natural resources.
For emergencies
Power cuts
Check your trip switch to make sure it's switched on. If it's
off, try switching it back on. If the switch moves back to
the off position you may have a fault with an appliance
or circuit in your property.
Do not touch any fuses or trip switches that show signs
of burning or if there is any black tar leaking. If this is the
case, or if your trip switch is on but you have no power
anywhere in your property then please call the emergency
number shown at the top of page 1.
Extending or Terminating your contract
If you're on a fixed or flexible contract, the 
Charges stated in your Contract Information 
Pack (CIP) will automatically end on the 
Earliest Termination Date shown in your CIP. 
You may write to us at any time up to the 
Earliest Termination Date to confirm that
you don't want to be transferred onto our
Extended Supply Charges and would like to
switch to a different supplier. If we don't 
hear from you by the Earliest Termination 
Date, you'll automatically move onto our 
Extended Supply Charges. You'll remain on
Extended Supply Charges until you either
agree another supply contract with us or
transfer to another supplier, which you can
do at no cost to you (as long as you have
no outstanding balance on your account). 
These Extended Supply Charges are variable
and typically higher than the charges found
in your CIP.
*All phone calls to and from us may be recorded for monitoring or training purposes. Calls to 0845 numbers will cost up to 7p per minute plus your standard network charges. 
The price of calls may vary with other operators and calls from mobiles may be considerably higher. Please check with your operator for exact charges.

How to pay
Direct Debit - it's the easiest way to pay
Setup a Direct Debit by downloading the
Direct Debit Instruction (DDI) from our website
edfenergy.com/billing. Fill this in and send it to:
Freepost EDF CUSTOMER CORRESPONDENCE
BACS or CHAPS 
You can transfer money straight into our account
either by BACS (Bankers Automated Clearing
System) or CHAPS (Clearing House Automated
Payment System). Our account details are as follows.
Account Name: EDF Energy Customers Ltd
Sort Code: 40-05-30
Account number: 44151844
Bank: HSBC Bank plc, 60 Queen Street, London,
EC4N 4TR.
IBAN Number: GB25MIDL40053044151844
SWIFT Code: MIDLGB22
If you want to pay by BACS or CHAPS, you need
to arrange this through your bank. Once you have
arranged the transfer, let us know by sending an
email to remittanceadvices@edfenergy.com. 
Your email must quote your 10-digit EDF Energy
account number shown on the front of this bill.
For more information on how to pay, visit
edfenergy.com/billing-payments
Be aware of scams that falsely tell you about a
change in payment details or payment
method. If you’re unsure, please get in touch
with us to check.
Debit card or credit card
To pay by debit card or credit card, phone the
revenue management team on 0845 302 7113*.
Please allow ten working days for your payment
to be processed.
At a bank or building society
You can pay by cash or cheque at a bank or building
society. It can take up to ten working days for
the payment to reach us.
Please note, you may have to pay a fee at some
banks and building societies.
Get in touch
Got a question about your bill?
If you'd like to know how your bill is calculated,
visit edfenergy.com/billing or call our
Customer Services on the number shown
on the front page of this bill.
MyBusiness
You can also register for our free
MyBusiness service on our website 
mybusinessaccount.edfenergy.com.
This service lets you check your account
information, download your bills, give us a meter
reading and get consumption data.
Making a complaint
Please get in touch with your dedicated
customer services team if you have a complaint.
They will do everything they can to put things
right as quickly as possible. Go to
edfenergy.com/complaints for our full
complaints procedure.
If you're a small business or microbusiness
customer
If your complaint isn't sorted after eight weeks
or you've received a 'deadlock' letter, you can
get in touch with the Energy Ombudsman. The
service is free and independent, and any
decision the Ombudsman makes is binding on
us - not you.
Call 0330 440 1624 (Monday to Friday
8am-6pm); email
enquiry@energyombudsman.org; Visit
energyombudsman.org
If you're a small or microbusiness customer who
has appointed a TPI, they'll be a member of a
qualifying dispute settlement service and can
direct you to that service. If you're unable to
contact your TPI, you can contact us at
edfenergy.com/complaints
More support
Citizens Advice and Advice Direct Scotland
If you're a small business or microbusiness
consumer, you can get help with energy problems.
This includes issues with your bills or meters,
or if you're struggling to pay for your energy.
If you live in England or Wales, go to
citizensadvice.org.uk/energy or contact the
Citizens Advice consumer service on 0808 223
1133. Calls are free.
If you live in Scotland, go to cas.org.uk, or
contact your Local Citizens Advice office. If you
live in Scotland, you can also contact Advice
Direct Scotland on 0808 196 8660 or visit
energyadvice.scot Calls are free.
If you need specialist support to resolve your
energy problem, Citizens Advice and Advice
Direct Scotland can refer you directly to the
Extra Help Unit Service.
Small business definition
• Fewer than 50 employees or their full-time
equivalent and an annual turnover of at most £6.5
million or a balance sheet total of £5.0 million. or
• An annual consumption of electricity of not
more than 200,000 kWh or
• An annual consumption of gas of not more than
500,000 kWh.
This document is fully recyclable
The paper used is made from 
50% recycled waste paper and
50% fibre from well-managed
natural resources.
Useful information
For more information on our products
and services, go to the website at
www.edfenergy.com/largebusiness.
Helping Britain's business and public sector
achieve Net Zero. For more information visit
edfenergy.com/net-zero-business
Site access
We take the safety of all our customers very
seriously. That’s why our representatives who visit
customers are highly trained and always carry
identification. If you’d like more information on
our obligations when visiting premises, phone us
on 0845 366 3664*.
Electricity and gas theft
Energy theft, carried out by interfering with
meters or connections, increases costs to our
customers. This means higher bills, which affects
everyone. Energy theft is a criminal offence
and can be extremely dangerous. If you suspect
someone is stealing electricity or gas, or both,
call the stayenergysafe reporting line on
0800 023 2777 or via stayenergysafe.co.uk. It
will be looked into urgently and your call can be
kept confidential. Alternatively, contact us directly
on 0333 200 5100.
Payment Processing Centre
PO Box 140
Plymouth
PL3 5RG
Page 6 of 7
There's another page with details of what to do if you have a powercut and our fuel mix detail.
Microbusiness definition
• An annual consumption of electricity of less than
  100,000 kWh or
• An annual consumption of gas of less than
  293,000 kWh or
• Fewer than the equivalent of ten full time
   employees and
• An annual turnover or annual balance sheet
   total not exceeding £2 million
Are you moving?
If you’re moving premises, let us know by filling in 
the form on our website
edfenergy.com/movingbusiness, or you can 
phone 0845 366 3664* for more advice.
Please have the MPAN or MPR numbers shown on 
your bill to hand so we can deal with your enquiry. 
You'll need to give us written notice at least 28 days 
before you move so that we can make sure your 
final bill is accurate. You'll also need to provide a
final meter reading and a forwarding address.
Do you need a new supply business 
connection?
The easiest way to set up a new connection is by 
filling in the form on our website
edfenergy.com/new-connection. Or email 
newsupply@edfenergy.com. Alternatively, call us 
on 0845 366 3664*.

The small print
Your Privacy
We respect your privacy and are committed to
providing you with a clear understanding of how we
use your data. To find out about the ways in which
we protect and use your data, please visit our Privacy
Notice and Policy at edfenergy.com/yourprivacy
If you’d like a hard copy of the privacy policy you can
contact us on 0333 200 5100 and we’ll post it to you.
VAT
Value added tax (VAT) is charged on your energy
bills . If you can claim VAT relief for domestic or
charitable non-business use, you need to fill in a
declaration form, which is available on our website.
For more information on VAT relief and the
declaration you must make, visit HMRC’s website
at HMRC.gov.uk.
Climate Change Levy (CCL)
Climate Change Levy (CCL) is a tax on the energy
used by businesses. It aims to encourage users to
be more energy efficient and reduce waste. Your
CCL charge will take account of any PP11 Supplier
certificate you have. Our CCL registration number
is: 523 0412 02 0000.
For more information on CCL, visit the website at
HMRC.gov.uk.
EDF Energy
EDF Energy is a trading name used by EDF Energy
Customers Ltd, Reg. No. 02228297 whose
Registered Office is at Nova North, 11 Bressenden Place,
London, SW1E 5BY, incorporated in England and Wales.
EDF Energy Customers Ltd is responsible for
meeting the supply obligations for all EDF supply
contracts.
Smart metering for business
We’re now introducing smart meters for our non
half-hourly business customers, which can help you
save time and provide more accurate bills from
automated meter readings. For more information,
visit edfenergy.com/smartmeter-business
Access to your consumption data
You can access your data by logging into your
MyBusiness account:
edfenergy.com/mybusiness
If your consumption data is not available, please
contact your customer service team (information on
top of your invoice).
By learning about your energy usage, you can
potentially lower your carbon footprint and your bills.
Payment Processing Centre
PO Box 140
Plymouth
PL3 5RG
Page 7 of 7
Our fuel mix   
Every year we must publish details of the fuel
sources that have been used to generate the 
electricity we supply to our customers.
The information in the table below covers our supply 
licence for EDF Energy Customers Ltd for the period 
from April 2024 to March 2025.
Our customers’ electricity is sourced from our own
UK power stations, the wholesale energy market
and other independent power generators. We
are a major supporter of independent renewable 
generators.
The figures for UK average fuel mix are provided by
the Department for Energy Security & Net Zero (DESNZ).
Depending on the tariff you are on, the
fuel source and carbon emissions associated with the
generation of your electricity may vary.
For more information on our fuel mix, visit 
edfenergy.com/fuelmix

fuel mix 4.2% 21.0% 54.8% 18.2% 1.8% 135 0.0038

This document is fully recyclable
The paper used is made from 
50% recycled waste paper and 
50% fibre from well-managed
natural resources.
For emergencies
Power cuts
Check your trip switch to make sure it's switched on. If it's
off, try switching it back on. If the switch moves back to
the off position you may have a fault with an appliance
or circuit in your property.
Do not touch any fuses or trip switches that show signs
of burning or if there is any black tar leaking. If this is the
case, or if your trip switch is on but you have no power
anywhere in your property then please call the emergency
number shown at the top of page 1.
Extending or Terminating your contract
If you're on a fixed or flexible contract, the 
Charges stated in your Contract Information 
Pack (CIP) will automatically end on the 
Earliest Termination Date shown in your CIP. 
You may write to us at any time up to the 
Earliest Termination Date to confirm that
you don't want to be transferred onto our
Extended Supply Charges and would like to
switch to a different supplier. If we don't 
hear from you by the Earliest Termination 
Date, you'll automatically move onto our 
Extended Supply Charges. You'll remain on
Extended Supply Charges until you either
agree another supply contract with us or
transfer to another supplier, which you can
do at no cost to you (as long as you have
no outstanding balance on your account). 
These Extended Supply Charges are variable
and typically higher than the charges found
in your CIP.
*All phone calls to and from us may be recorded for monitoring or training purposes. Calls to 0845 numbers will cost up to 7p per minute plus your standard network charges. 
The price of calls may vary with other operators and calls from mobiles may be considerably higher. Please check with your operator for exact charges.

How to pay
Direct Debit - it's the easiest way to pay
Setup a Direct Debit by downloading the
Direct Debit Instruction (DDI) from our website
edfenergy.com/billing. Fill this in and send it to:
Freepost EDF CUSTOMER CORRESPONDENCE
BACS or CHAPS 
You can transfer money straight into our account
either by BACS (Bankers Automated Clearing
System) or CHAPS (Clearing House Automated
Payment System). Our account details are as follows.
Account Name: EDF Energy Customers Ltd
Sort Code: 40-05-30
Account number: 44151844
Bank: HSBC Bank plc, 60 Queen Street, London,
EC4N 4TR.
IBAN Number: GB25MIDL40053044151844
SWIFT Code: MIDLGB22
If you want to pay by BACS or CHAPS, you need
to arrange this through your bank. Once you have
arranged the transfer, let us know by sending an
email to remittanceadvices@edfenergy.com. 
Your email must quote your 10-digit EDF Energy
account number shown on the front of this bill.
For more information on how to pay, visit
edfenergy.com/billing-payments
Be aware of scams that falsely tell you about a
change in payment details or payment
method. If you’re unsure, please get in touch
with us to check.
Debit card or credit card
To pay by debit card or credit card, phone the
revenue management team on 0845 302 7113*.
Please allow ten working days for your payment
to be processed.
At a bank or building society
You can pay by cash or cheque at a bank or building
society. It can take up to ten working days for
the payment to reach us.
Please note, you may have to pay a fee at some
banks and building societies.
Get in touch
Got a question about your bill?
If you'd like to know how your bill is calculated,
visit edfenergy.com/billing or call our
Customer Services on the number shown
on the front page of this bill.
MyBusiness
You can also register for our free
MyBusiness service on our website 
mybusinessaccount.edfenergy.com.
This service lets you check your account
information, download your bills, give us a meter
reading and get consumption data.
Making a complaint
Please get in touch with your dedicated
customer services team if you have a complaint.
They will do everything they can to put things
right as quickly as possible. Go to
edfenergy.com/complaints for our full
complaints procedure.
If you're a small business or microbusiness
customer
If your complaint isn't sorted after eight weeks
or you've received a 'deadlock' letter, you can
get in touch with the Energy Ombudsman. The
service is free and independent, and any
decision the Ombudsman makes is binding on
us - not you.
Call 0330 440 1624 (Monday to Friday
8am-6pm); email
enquiry@energyombudsman.org; Visit
energyombudsman.org
If you're a small or microbusiness customer who
has appointed a TPI, they'll be a member of a
qualifying dispute settlement service and can
direct you to that service. If you're unable to
contact your TPI, you can contact us at
edfenergy.com/complaints
More support
Citizens Advice and Advice Direct Scotland
If you're a small business or microbusiness
consumer, you can get help with energy problems.
This includes issues with your bills or meters,
or if you're struggling to pay for your energy.
If you live in England or Wales, go to
citizensadvice.org.uk/energy or contact the
Citizens Advice consumer service on 0808 223
1133. Calls are free.
If you live in Scotland, go to cas.org.uk, or
contact your Local Citizens Advice office. If you
live in Scotland, you can also contact Advice
Direct Scotland on 0808 196 8660 or visit
energyadvice.scot Calls are free.
If you need specialist support to resolve your
energy problem, Citizens Advice and Advice
Direct Scotland can refer you directly to the
Extra Help Unit Service.
Small business definition
• Fewer than 50 employees or their full-time
equivalent and an annual turnover of at most £6.5
million or a balance sheet total of £5.0 million. or
• An annual consumption of electricity of not
more than 200,000 kWh or
• An annual consumption of gas of not more than
500,000 kWh.

natural resources.
Useful information

and services, go to the website at
www.edfenergy.com/largebusiness.
Helping Britain's business and public sector
achieve Net Zero. For more information visit
edfenergy.com/net-zero-business
Site access
We take the safety of all our customers very
seriously. That’s why our representatives who visit
customers are highly trained and always carry
identification. If you’d like more information on
our obligations when visiting premises, phone us
on 0845 366 3664*.
Electricity and gas theft
Energy theft, carried out by interfering with
meters or connections, increases costs to our
customers. This means higher bills, which affects
everyone. Energy theft is a criminal offence
and can be extremely dangerous. If you suspect
someone is stealing electricity or gas, or both,
call the stayenergysafe reporting line on
0800 023 2777 or via stayenergysafe.co.uk. It
will be looked into urgently and your call can be
kept confidential. Alternatively, contact us directly
on 0333 200 5100.

PO Box 140
Plymouth
PL3 5RG

Microbusiness definition
• An annual consumption of electricity of less than
  100,000 kWh or
• An annual consumption of gas of less than
  293,000 kWh or
• Fewer than the equivalent of ten full time
   employees and
• An annual turnover or annual balance sheet
   total not exceeding £2 million
Are you moving?
If you’re moving premises, let us know by filling in 
the form on our website
edfenergy.com/movingbusiness, or you can 
phone 0845 366 3664* for more advice.
Please have the MPAN or MPR numbers shown on 
your bill to hand so we can deal with your enquiry. 
You'll need to give us written notice at least 28 days 
before you move so that we can make sure your 
final bill is accurate. You'll also need to provide a
final meter reading and a forwarding address.
Do you need a new supply business 
connection?
The easiest way to set up a new connection is by 
filling in the form on our website
edfenergy.com/new-connection. Or email 
newsupply@edfenergy.com. Alternatively, call us 
on 0845 366 3664*.

The small print
Your Privacy
We respect your privacy and are committed to
providing you with a clear understanding of how we
use your data. To find out about the ways in which
we protect and use your data, please visit our Privacy
Notice and Policy at edfenergy.com/yourprivacy
If you’d like a hard copy of the privacy policy you can
contact us on 0333 200 5100 and we’ll post it to you.
VAT
Value added tax (VAT) is charged on your energy
bills . If you can claim VAT relief for domestic or
charitable non-business use, you need to fill in a
declaration form, which is available on our website.
For more information on VAT relief and the
declaration you must make, visit HMRC’s website
at HMRC.gov.uk.
Climate Change Levy (CCL)
Climate Change Levy (CCL) is a tax on the energy
used by businesses. It aims to encourage users to
be more energy efficient and reduce waste. Your
CCL charge will take account of any PP11 Supplier
certificate you have. Our CCL registration number
is: 523 0412 02 0000.
For more information on CCL, visit the website at
HMRC.gov.uk.
EDF Energy
EDF Energy is a trading name used by EDF Energy
Customers Ltd, Reg. No. 02228297 whose
Registered Office is at Nova North, 11 Bressenden Place,
London, SW1E 5BY, incorporated in England and Wales.
EDF Energy Customers Ltd is responsible for
meeting the supply obligations for all EDF supply
contracts.
Smart metering for business
We’re now introducing smart meters for our non
half-hourly business customers, which can help you
save time and provide more accurate bills from
automated meter readings. For more information,
visit edfenergy.com/smartmeter-business
Access to your consumption data
You can access your data by logging into your
MyBusiness account:
edfenergy.com/mybusiness
If your consumption data is not available, please
contact your customer service team (information on
top of your invoice).
By learning about your energy usage, you can
potentially lower your carbon footprint and your bills.

Our fuel mix   
Every year we must publish details of the fuel
sources that have been used to generate the 
electricity we supply to our customers.
The information in the table below covers our supply 
licence for EDF Energy Customers Ltd for the period 
from April 2024 to March 2025.
Our customers’ electricity is sourced from our own
UK power stations, the wholesale energy market
and other independent power generators. We
are a major supporter of independent renewable 
generators.
The figures for UK average fuel mix are provided by
the Department for Energy Security & Net Zero (DESNZ).
Depending on the tariff you are on, the
fuel source and carbon emissions associated with the
generation of your electricity may vary.

For emergencies
Power cuts
Check your trip switch to make sure it's switched on. If it's
off, try switching it back on. If the switch moves back to
the off position you may have a fault with an appliance
or circuit in your property.
Do not touch any fuses or trip switches that show signs
of burning or if there is any black tar leaking. If this is the
case, or if your trip switch is on but you have no power
anywhere in your property then please call the emergency
number shown at the top of page 1.
Extending or Terminating your contract
If you're on a fixed or flexible contract, the 
Charges stated in your Contract Information 
Pack (CIP) will automatically end on the 
Earliest Termination Date shown in your CIP. 
You may write to us at any time up to the 
Earliest Termination Date to confirm that
you don't want to be transferred onto our
Extended Supply Charges and would like to
switch to a different supplier. If we don't 
hear from you by the Earliest Termination 
Date, you'll automatically move onto our 
Extended Supply Charges. You'll remain on
Extended Supply Charges until you either
agree another supply contract with us or
transfer to another supplier, which you can
do at no cost to you (as long as you have
no outstanding balance on your account). 
These Extended Supply Charges are variable
and typically higher than the charges found
in your CIP.
*All phone calls to and from us may be recorded for monitoring or training purposes. Calls to 0845 numbers will cost up to 7p per minute plus your standard network charges. 
The price of calls may vary with other operators and calls from mobiles may be considerably higher. Please check with your operator for exact charges.

If you’d like a hard copy of the privacy policy you can
contact us on 0333 200 5100 and we’ll post it to you.
VAT
Value added tax (VAT) is charged on your energy
bills . If you can claim VAT relief for domestic or
charitable non-business use, you need to fill in a
declaration form, which is available on our website.
For more information on VAT relief and the
declaration you must make, visit HMRC’s website
at HMRC.gov.uk.
Climate Change Levy (CCL)
Climate Change Levy (CCL) is a tax on the energy
used by businesses. It aims to encourage users to
be more energy efficient and reduce waste. Your
CCL charge will take account of any PP11 Supplier
certificate you have. Our CCL registration number
is: 523 0412 02 0000.
For more information on CCL, visit the website at
HMRC.gov.uk.
EDF Energy
EDF Energy is a trading name used by EDF Energy
Customers Ltd, Reg. No. 02228297 whose
Registered Office is at Nova North, 11 Bressenden Place,
London, SW1E 5BY, incorporated in England and Wales.
EDF Energy Customers Ltd is responsible for
meeting the supply obligations for all EDF supply
contracts.
Smart metering for business
We’re now introducing smart meters for our non
half-hourly business customers, which can help you
save time and provide more accurate bills from
automated meter readings. For more information,
visit edfenergy.com/smartmeter-business
Access to your consumption data
You can access your data by logging into your
MyBusiness account:
edfenergy.com/mybusiness
If your consumption data is not available, please
contact your customer service team (information on
top of your invoice).
By learning about your energy usage, you can
potentially lower your carbon footprint and your bills.
Payment Processing Centre
PO Box 140
Plymouth
PL3 5RG
Page 7 of 7
Our fuel mix   
Every year we must publish details of the fuel
sources that have been used to generate the 
electricity we supply to our customers.
The information in the table below covers our supply 
licence for EDF Energy Customers Ltd for the period 
from April 2024 to March 2025.
Our customers’ electricity is sourced from our own
UK power stations, the wholesale energy market
and other independent power generators. We
are a major supporter of independent renewable 
generators.
The figures for UK average fuel mix are provided by
the Department for Energy Security & Net Zero (DESNZ).
Depending on the tariff you are on, the
fuel source and carbon emissions associated with the
generation of your electricity may vary.
For more information on our fuel mix, visit 
edfenergy.com/fuelmix

This document is fully recyclable
The paper used is made from 
50% recycled waste paper and 
50% fibre from well-managed
natural resources.
For emergencies
Power cuts
Check your trip switch to make sure it's switched on. If it's
off, try switching it back on. If the switch moves back to
the off position you may have a fault with an appliance
or circuit in your property.
Do not touch any fuses or trip switches that show signs
of burning or if there is any black tar leaking. If this is the
case, or if your trip switch is on but you have no power
anywhere in your property then please call the emergency
number shown at the top of page 1.
Extending or Terminating your contract
If you're on a fixed or flexible contract, the 
Charges stated in your Contract Information 
Pack (CIP) will automatically end on the 
Earliest Termination Date shown in your CIP. 
You may write to us at any time up to the 
Earliest Termination Date to confirm that
you don't want to be transferred onto our
Extended Supply Charges and would like to
switch to a different supplier. If we don't 
hear from you by the Earliest Termination 
Date, you'll automatically move onto our 
Extended Supply Charges. You'll remain on
Extended Supply Charges until you either
agree another supply contract with us or
transfer to another supplier, which you can
do at no cost to you (as long as you have
no outstanding balance on your account). 
These Extended Supply Charges are variable
and typically higher than the charges found
in your CIP.
*All phone calls to and from us may be recorded for monitoring or training purposes. Calls to 0845 numbers will cost up to 7p per minute plus your standard network charges. 
The price of calls may vary with other operators and calls from mobiles may be considerably higher. Please check with your operator for exact charges.

How to pay
Direct Debit - it's the easiest way to pay
Setup a Direct Debit by downloading the
Direct Debit Instruction (DDI) from our website
edfenergy.com/billing. Fill this in and send it to:
Freepost EDF CUSTOMER CORRESPONDENCE
BACS or CHAPS 
You can transfer money straight into our account
either by BACS (Bankers Automated Clearing
System) or CHAPS (Clearing House Automated
Payment System). Our account details are as follows.
Account Name: EDF Energy Customers Ltd
Sort Code: 40-05-30
Account number: 44151844
Bank: HSBC Bank plc, 60 Queen Street, London,
EC4N 4TR.
IBAN Number: GB25MIDL40053044151844
SWIFT Code: MIDLGB22
If you want to pay by BACS or CHAPS, you need
to arrange this through your bank. Once you have
arranged the transfer, let us know by sending an
email to remittanceadvices@edfenergy.com. 
Your email must quote your 10-digit EDF Energy
account number shown on the front of this bill.
For more information on how to pay, visit
edfenergy.com/billing-payments
Be aware of scams that falsely tell you about a
change in payment details or payment
method. If you’re unsure, please get in touch
with us to check.
Debit card or credit card
To pay by debit card or credit card, phone the
revenue management team on 0845 302 7113*.
Please allow ten working days for your payment
to be processed.
At a bank or building society
You can pay by cash or cheque at a bank or building
society. It can take up to ten working days for
the payment to reach us.
Please note, you may have to pay a fee at some
banks and building societies.
Get in touch
Got a question about your bill?
If you'd like to know how your bill is calculated,
visit edfenergy.com/billing or call our
Customer Services on the number shown
on the front page of this bill.
MyBusiness
You can also register for our free
MyBusiness service on our website 
mybusinessaccount.edfenergy.com.
This service lets you check your account
information, download your bills, give us a meter
reading and get consumption data.
Making a complaint
Please get in touch with your dedicated
customer services team if you have a complaint.
They will do everything they can to put things
right as quickly as possible. Go to
edfenergy.com/complaints for our full
complaints procedure.
If you're a small business or microbusiness
customer
If your complaint isn't sorted after eight weeks
or you've received a 'deadlock' letter, you can
get in touch with the Energy Ombudsman. The
service is free and independent, and any
decision the Ombudsman makes is binding on
us - not you.
Call 0330 440 1624 (Monday to Friday
8am-6pm); email
enquiry@energyombudsman.org; Visit
energyombudsman.org
If you're a small or microbusiness customer who
has appointed a TPI, they'll be a member of a
qualifying dispute settlement service and can
direct you to that service. If you're unable to
contact your TPI, you can contact us at
edfenergy.com/complaints
More support
Citizens Advice and Advice Direct Scotland
If you're a small business or microbusiness
consumer, you can get help with energy problems.
This includes issues with your bills or meters,
or if you're struggling to pay for your energy.
If you live in England or Wales, go to
citizensadvice.org.uk/energy or contact the
Citizens Advice consumer service on 0808 223
1133. Calls are free.
If you live in Scotland, go to cas.org.uk, or
contact your Local Citizens Advice office. If you
live in Scotland, you can also contact Advice
Direct Scotland on 0808 196 8660 or visit
energyadvice.scot Calls are free.
If you need specialist support to resolve your
energy problem, Citizens Advice and Advice
Direct Scotland can refer you directly to the
Extra Help Unit Service.
Small business definition
• Fewer than 50 employees or their full-time
equivalent and an annual turnover of at most £6.5
million or a balance sheet total of £5.0 million. or
• An annual consumption of electricity of not
more than 200,000 kWh or
• An annual consumption of gas of not more than
500,000 kWh.
This document is fully recyclable
The paper used is made from 
50% recycled waste paper and
50% fibre from well-managed
natural resources.
Useful information
For more information on our products
and services, go to the website at
www.edfenergy.com/largebusiness.
Helping Britain's business and public sector
achieve Net Zero. For more information visit
edfenergy.com/net-zero-business
Site access
We take the safety of all our customers very
seriously. That’s why our representatives who visit
customers are highly trained and always carry
identification. If you’d like more information on
our obligations when visiting premises, phone us
on 0845 366 3664*.
Electricity and gas theft
Energy theft, carried out by interfering with
meters or connections, increases costs to our
customers. This means higher bills, which affects
everyone. Energy theft is a criminal offence
and can be extremely dangerous. If you suspect
someone is stealing electricity or gas, or both,
call the stayenergysafe reporting line on
0800 023 2777 or via stayenergysafe.co.uk. It
will be looked into urgently and your call can be
kept confidential. Alternatively, contact us directly
on 0333 200 5100.
Payment Processing Centre
PO Box 140
Plymouth
PL3 5RG
Page 7 of 8
There's another page with details of what to do if you have a powercut and our fuel mix detail.
Microbusiness definition
• An annual consumption of electricity of less than
  100,000 kWh or
• An annual consumption of gas of less than
  293,000 kWh or
• Fewer than the equivalent of ten full time
   employees and
• An annual turnover or annual balance sheet
   total not exceeding £2 million
Are you moving?
If you’re moving premises, let us know by filling in 
the form on our website
edfenergy.com/movingbusiness, or you can 
phone 0845 366 3664* for more advice.
Please have the MPAN or MPR numbers shown on 
your bill to hand so we can deal with your enquiry. 
You'll need to give us written notice at least 28 days 
before you move so that we can make sure your 
final bill is accurate. You'll also need to provide a
final meter reading and a forwarding address.
Do you need a new supply business 
connection?
The easiest way to set up a new connection is by 
filling in the form on our website
edfenergy.com/new-connection. Or email 
newsupply@edfenergy.com. Alternatively, call us 
on 0845 366 3664*.

The small print
Your Privacy
We respect your privacy and are committed to
providing you with a clear understanding of how we
use your data. To find out about the ways in which
we protect and use your data, please visit our Privacy
Notice and Policy at edfenergy.com/yourprivacy
If you’d like a hard copy of the privacy policy you can
contact us on 0333 200 5100 and we’ll post it to you.
VAT
Value added tax (VAT) is charged on your energy
bills . If you can claim VAT relief for domestic or
charitable non-business use, you need to fill in a
declaration form, which is available on our website.
For more information on VAT relief and the
declaration you must make, visit HMRC’s website
at HMRC.gov.uk.
Climate Change Levy (CCL)
Climate Change Levy (CCL) is a tax on the energy
used by businesses. It aims to encourage users to
be more energy efficient and reduce waste. Your
CCL charge will take account of any PP11 Supplier
certificate you have. Our CCL registration number
is: 523 0412 02 0000.
For more information on CCL, visit the website at
HMRC.gov.uk.
EDF Energy
EDF Energy is a trading name used by EDF Energy
Customers Ltd, Reg. No. 02228297 whose
Registered Office is at Nova North, 11 Bressenden Place,
London, SW1E 5BY, incorporated in England and Wales.
EDF Energy Customers Ltd is responsible for
meeting the supply obligations for all EDF supply
contracts.
Smart metering for business
We’re now introducing smart meters for our non
half-hourly business customers, which can help you
save time and provide more accurate bills from
automated meter readings. For more information,
visit edfenergy.com/smartmeter-business
Access to your consumption data
You can access your data by logging into your
MyBusiness account:
edfenergy.com/mybusiness
If your consumption data is not available, please
contact your customer service team (information on
top of your invoice).
By learning about your energy usage, you can
potentially lower your carbon footprint and your bills.

Our fuel mix   
Every year we must publish details of the fuel
sources that have been used to generate the 
electricity we supply to our customers.
The information in the table below covers our supply 
licence for EDF Energy Customers Ltd for the period 
from April 2024 to March 2025.
Our customers’ electricity is sourced from our own
UK power stations, the wholesale energy market
and other independent power generators. We
are a major supporter of independent renewable 
generators.
The figures for UK average fuel mix are provided by
the Department for Energy Security & Net Zero (DESNZ).
Depending on the tariff you are on, the
fuel source and carbon emissions associated with the
generation of your electricity may vary.
For more information on our fuel mix, visit 
edfenergy.com/fuelmix

For emergencies
Power cuts
Check your trip switch to make sure it's switched on. If it's
off, try switching it back on. If the switch moves back to
the off position you may have a fault with an appliance
or circuit in your property.
Do not touch any fuses or trip switches that show signs
of burning or if there is any black tar leaking. If this is the
case, or if your trip switch is on but you have no power
anywhere in your property then please call the emergency
number shown at the top of page 1.
Extending or Terminating your contract
If you're on a fixed or flexible contract, the 
Charges stated in your Contract Information 
Pack (CIP) will automatically end on the 
Earliest Termination Date shown in your CIP. 
You may write to us at any time up to the 
Earliest Termination Date to confirm that
you don't want to be transferred onto our
Extended Supply Charges and would like to
switch to a different supplier. If we don't 
hear from you by the Earliest Termination 
Date, you'll automatically move onto our 
Extended Supply Charges. You'll remain on
Extended Supply Charges until you either
agree another supply contract with us or
transfer to another supplier, which you can
do at no cost to you (as long as you have
no outstanding balance on your account). 
These Extended Supply Charges are variable
and typically higher than the charges found
in your CIP.
*All phone calls to and from us may be recorded for monitoring or training purposes. Calls to 0845 numbers will cost up to 7p per minute plus your standard network charges. 
The price of calls may vary with other operators and calls from mobiles may be considerably higher. Please check with your operator for exact charges.

How to pay
Direct Debit - it's the easiest way to pay
Setup a Direct Debit by downloading the
Direct Debit Instruction (DDI) from our website
edfenergy.com/billing. Fill this in and send it to:
Freepost EDF CUSTOMER CORRESPONDENCE
BACS or CHAPS 
You can transfer money straight into our account
either by BACS (Bankers Automated Clearing
System) or CHAPS (Clearing House Automated
Payment System). Our account details are as follows.
Account Name: EDF Energy Customers Ltd
Sort Code: 40-05-30
Account number: 44151844
Bank: HSBC Bank plc, 60 Queen Street, London,
EC4N 4TR.
IBAN Number: GB25MIDL40053044151844
SWIFT Code: MIDLGB22
If you want to pay by BACS or CHAPS, you need
to arrange this through your bank. Once you have
arranged the transfer, let us know by sending an
email to remittanceadvices@edfenergy.com. 
Your email must quote your 10-digit EDF Energy
account number shown on the front of this bill.
For more information on how to pay, visit
edfenergy.com/billing-payments
Be aware of scams that falsely tell you about a
change in payment details or payment
method. If you’re unsure, please get in touch
with us to check.
Debit card or credit card
To pay by debit card or credit card, phone the
revenue management team on 0845 302 7113*.
Please allow ten working days for your payment
to be processed.
At a bank or building society
You can pay by cash or cheque at a bank or building
society. It can take up to ten working days for
the payment to reach us.
Please note, you may have to pay a fee at some
banks and building societies.
Get in touch
Got a question about your bill?
If you'd like to know how your bill is calculated,
visit edfenergy.com/billing or call our
Customer Services on the number shown
on the front page of this bill.
MyBusiness
You can also register for our free
MyBusiness service on our website 
mybusinessaccount.edfenergy.com.
This service lets you check your account
information, download your bills, give us a meter
reading and get consumption data.
Making a complaint
Please get in touch with your dedicated
customer services team if you have a complaint.
They will do everything they can to put things
right as quickly as possible. Go to
edfenergy.com/complaints for our full
complaints procedure.
If you're a small business or microbusiness
customer
If your complaint isn't sorted after eight weeks
or you've received a 'deadlock' letter, you can
get in touch with the Energy Ombudsman. The
service is free and independent, and any
decision the Ombudsman makes is binding on
us - not you.
Call 0330 440 1624 (Monday to Friday
8am-6pm); email
enquiry@energyombudsman.org; Visit
energyombudsman.org
If you're a small or microbusiness customer who
has appointed a TPI, they'll be a member of a
qualifying dispute settlement service and can
direct you to that service. If you're unable to
contact your TPI, you can contact us at
edfenergy.com/complaints
More support
Citizens Advice and Advice Direct Scotland
If you're a small business or microbusiness
consumer, you can get help with energy problems.
This includes issues with your bills or meters,
or if you're struggling to pay for your energy.
If you live in England or Wales, go to
citizensadvice.org.uk/energy or contact the
Citizens Advice consumer service on 0808 223
1133. Calls are free.
If you live in Scotland, go to cas.org.uk, or
contact your Local Citizens Advice office. If you
live in Scotland, you can also contact Advice
Direct Scotland on 0808 196 8660 or visit
energyadvice.scot Calls are free.
If you need specialist support to resolve your
energy problem, Citizens Advice and Advice
Direct Scotland can refer you directly to the
Extra Help Unit Service.

• Fewer than 50 employees or their full-time
equivalent and an annual turnover of at most £6.5
million or a balance sheet total of £5.0 million. or
• An annual consumption of electricity of not
more than 200,000 kWh or
• An annual consumption of gas of not more than
500,000 kWh.

electricity we supply to our customers.
The information in the table below covers our supply 
licence for EDF Energy Customers Ltd for the period 
from April 2024 to March 2025.
Our customers’ electricity is sourced from our own
UK power stations, the wholesale energy market
and other independent power generators. We
are a major supporter of independent renewable 
generators.
The figures for UK average fuel mix are provided by
the Department for Energy Security & Net Zero (DESNZ).
Depending on the tariff you are on, the
fuel source and carbon emissions associated with the
generation of your electricity may vary.
For more information on our fuel mix, visit 
edfenergy.com/fuelmix
 
EDF's
fuel mix 4.2% 21.0% 54.8% 18.2% 1.8% 135 0.0038
Contribution to our
carbon emissions 28.3% 59.3% 0.0% 0.0% 12.4%

This document is fully recyclable
The paper used is made from 
50% recycled waste paper and 
50% fibre from well-managed
natural resources.
For emergencies
Power cuts
Check your trip switch to make sure it's switched on. If it's
off, try switching it back on. If the switch moves back to
the off position you may have a fault with an appliance
or circuit in your property.
Do not touch any fuses or trip switches that show signs
of burning or if there is any black tar leaking. If this is the
case, or if your trip switch is on but you have no power
anywhere in your property then please call the emergency
number shown at the top of page 1.
Extending or Terminating your contract
If you're on a fixed or flexible contract, the 
Charges stated in your Contract Information 
Pack (CIP) will automatically end on the 
Earliest Termination Date shown in your CIP. 
You may write to us at any time up to the 
Earliest Termination Date to confirm that
you don't want to be transferred onto our
Extended Supply Charges and would like to
switch to a different supplier. If we don't 
hear from you by the Earliest Termination 
Date, you'll automatically move onto our 
Extended Supply Charges. You'll remain on
Extended Supply Charges until you either
agree another supply contract with us or
transfer to another supplier, which you can
do at no cost to you (as long as you have
no outstanding balance on your account). 
These Extended Supply Charges are variable
and typically higher than the charges found
in your CIP.
*All phone calls to and from us may be recorded for monitoring or training purposes. Calls to 0845 numbers will cost up to 7p per minute plus your standard network charges. 
The price of calls may vary with other operators and calls from mobiles may be considerably higher. Please check with your operator for exact charges.
